mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls confirms strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 momentum over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 1-year rolling windows, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive PAYEMS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When real sales of autos and light trucks cross above 12 million dollars, consumer demand is sufficiently robust to sustain corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production crossing below 95 signals a contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO falls below 95, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines, the economy enters a late-cycle tightening phase where higher borrowing costs outweigh labor market strength, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS MoM change > 0 and UNRATE MoM change < 0
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of occurrences over a 5-year sample, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized strength in construction and labor markets, which amplifies economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 300, rising Fed funds rates successfully anchor inflation expectations and reduce real discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in <45% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the regime-specific coefficient is negative and significant.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary regime where real GDP declines quarter-over-quarter, a rebound in real auto sales signals demand stabilization and bottoming earnings, producing positive S&P 500 momentum over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months following a negative GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter print, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
